topic.name: Historical Battleships
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical battleships from different navies, focusing on their design, operational history, and significance in naval warfare.

Document 1: Imperator Aleksandr II-class battleships were built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the 1880s.
They were designed according to the tactical theories of the day which emphasized ramming.
Document 2: Vittorio Emanuele was an Italian pre-dreadnought battleship, laid down in 1901.
She served during the First World War, but saw no combat during the war.
Document 3: Gneisenau was a German capital ship of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
Gneisenau and Scharnhorst operated together for much of the early portion of World War II.

topic.name: State Highways in North America
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in North America, detailing their routes, historical significance, and geographical context.

Document 1: M-77 is a state trunkline highway in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan.
The highway borders the eastern edge of the Seney National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 2: King's Highway 417, commonly referred to as Highway 417, is a 400-series highway in Ontario.
It connects Montreal with Ottawa, forming part of the transportation system in the National Capital Region.
Document 3: State Route 369 is a short, two-lane north-south state highway in Ohio.
SR 369 serves as a connector between SR 4 and US 40 southwest of Springfield.

topic.name: Atlantic Tropical Storms
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical storms and hurricanes that occurred during the Atlantic hurricane seasons, detailing their formation, impacts, and meteorological histories.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Elena was a weak tropical storm that moved ashore along Texas in the 1979 Atlantic hurricane season.
Elena dropped moderate rainfall along its path, causing two direct deaths in Houston from drowning.
Document 2: Hurricane Cindy was a tropical cyclone that briefly reached minimal hurricane strength in the Gulf of Mexico during July in the 2005 Atlantic hurricane season.
Cindy was responsible for 3 deaths in the United States and brought heavy rains to Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama and Maryland.
Document 3: Tropical Storm Delta was a late-forming tropical storm of the 2005 Atlantic hurricane season which struck the Canary Islands as a strong extratropical storm.
It was the twenty-sixth tropical or subtropical storm of the season, making the 2005 season the first to record so many storms.

topic.name: Major League Baseball Pitchers
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers and achievements of various pitchers in Major League Baseball.

Document 1: Monte Pearson was an American baseball pitcher who played ten seasons in Major League Baseball.
He is most famous for pitching the first no-hitter at the original Yankee Stadium.
Document 2: Russell Ormand Christopher was an American right-handed pitcher in Major League Baseball who played for seven seasons.
He made his only All-Star appearance in 1945.
Document 3: Guillermo Mota is a former Dominican professional baseball relief pitcher in Major League Baseball.
Mota pitched for several teams including the Montreal Expos and Los Angeles Dodgers.

topic.name: Biodiversity of Unique Species
topic.description: This topic encompasses the diversity and characteristics of unique species across different biological classifications, including marine life and fungi.

Document 1: Zebra shark (Stegostoma fasciatum) is a species of carpet shark and the sole member of the family Stegostomatidae.
Zebra sharks are nocturnal and spend most of the day resting motionless on the sea floor.
Document 2: Boletopsis nothofagi is a fungus in the family Bankeraceae.
The fungus is most likely a native species of New Zealand and was present there before the arrival of Europeans.
Document 3: Wynnea americana, commonly known as moose antlers or rabbit ears, is a species of fungus in the family Sarcoscyphaceae.
The fruit bodies grow clustered together from large underground masses of compacted mycelia known as sclerotia.

topic.name: Early Islamic Military History
topic.description: The documents discuss significant battles and events during the early period of Islamic expansion and the establishment of key religious sites.

Document 1: The Battle of the Baggage was fought between the forces of the Umayyad Caliphate and the Turkic Turgesh tribes in September / October 737.
The failure of the Umayyad campaign meant the complete collapse of the Arab control in the Upper Oxus valley.
Document 2: Al-Masjid an-Nabawi was the second mosque built in the history of Islam and is now one of the largest mosques in the world.

Document 3: The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, March 23, 625 at the valley located in front of Mount Uhud.

Document 2: The 2000 Malaysian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 22 October 2000 at the Sepang International Circuit.

topic.name: Mathematical Concepts and Theories
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical concepts, including number theory, electrical network properties, and mathematical constants.

Document 1: Znám 's problem asks which sets of k integers have the property that each integer in the set is a proper divisor of the product of the other integers in the set, plus 1.
It is known that there are only finitely many solutions for any fixed k.
Document 2: An antimetric electrical network is an electrical network that exhibits anti-symmetrical electrical properties.
References to symmetry and antimetry of a network usually refer to the input impedances of a two-port network when correctly terminated.
Document 3: The number e is an important mathematical constant that is the base of the natural logarithm.
The function f ( x ) = ex is called the exponential function, and its inverse is the natural logarithm.

topic.name: Legal Framework in Singapore
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal statutes and notable legal figures in Singapore, focusing on laws related to internal security and the legal profession.

Document 1: The Internal Security Act ( ISA ) of Singapore is a statute that grants the executive power to enforce preventive detention, prevent subversion, suppress organized violence against persons and property.
Before a person can be detained under the ISA by the Minister for Home Affairs, the President must be satisfied that such detention is necessary for the purposes of national security or public order.
Document 2: Glenn Jeyasingam Knight was the first Director of the Commercial Affairs Department ( CAD ) when it was founded in 1984.
He joined the Singapore Legal Service in 1970 and rose through the ranks.

Document 2: The Talbot Samba was a supermini car manufactured by the PSA Group.

Document 3: The Heinkel He 111 was designed as a fast medium bomber for the Luftwaffe.
